evidence_excerpt: The disorder is described as progressive, with muscular involvement starting cranially and descending with increasing disease duration. Follow-up noted new deficits appearing two years later in individual cases (e.g., EMG becoming myopathic; new loss of Achilles reflexes and distal vibration sense).

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Persistent ocular involvement (ptosis/PEO), discomfort with lateral gaze or nystagmus in younger patients, and neuromuscular signs (weakness, sensory loss) were ongoing; however, dependence or major impairment was not reported.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: In one patient, EMG repeated two years later showed new myopathic changes in the frontalis muscle (progression from previously normal EMG).
A patient with initially normal tendon reflexes and sensory findings later (two years after baseline) developed absent Achilles tendon reflexes and diminished distal vibration sense.

notes_on_uncertainty: The source describes a family cohort rather than a single patient. Index time was approximated as the initial specialist evaluations performed in the study. Long-term outcomes are inferred from described progression over years and the few documented two-year follow-ups; exact dates and individual-level functional trajectories are limited.
